SCMOREACTIVITIES.Module.Title: SC - More Activities
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Module.Description: A Shattered Codex activity platform for the dnd5e system.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.DebugLogging.H: Log SC - More Activities lifecycle and diagnostic messages to the browser console.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.SupportMenu.Hi: Support Shattered Codex development on Patreon.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.DocumentationM: Open the SC - More Activities documentation wiki.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.ActivityCatalo: Open the registered activity catalog and diagnostics.
Stores this user's preview colors for teleport, movement, and wall overlays.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.PreviewColorsM: Choose the colors used by teleport, movement, and wall previews for this user.
These colors affect the live preview overlays shown by teleport, movement, and wall tools for your user.
Changes update immediately in these sample overlays.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.DisabledActivi: Activity type IDs blocked from creation and use.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Title: Activity Catalog
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Summary.Label: Activity registration summary
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Tabs.Label: Activity catalog sections
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Columns.Label: Label
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Availability.Ac: Available for activity creation and use.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Availability.Di: Blocked from activity creation and use.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Availability.Un: Only D&D-ready activity types can be enabled or disabled.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Notifications.R: Could not copy the activity registration report.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Catalog.Notifications.A: {label} enabled for activity creation and use.
{label} disabled for activity creation and use.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Settings.MigrationBacku: Stores legacy more-activities migration backups for restore.
How many explicit migration backups to keep in world settings.
Preview, apply, export, and restore explicit migrations from the legacy more-activities module.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Title: More Activities Migration
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Tabs.Label: Migration sections
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Warning.Legac: The legacy more-activities module is still active. Keep both modules enabled only during preview or migration.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Warning.Previ: Run a preview before applying the migration.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Info.ApplyCom: Updated {items} item(s) and converted {activities} activity entries.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Empty.Preview: No legacy more-activities entries were found in the current preview.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Empty.Apply: No migration apply report is available yet.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Empty.Restore: No restore report is available yet.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Empty.Backups: No migration backups are available yet.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Error.GmOnly: Only a GM can preview, apply, or restore more-activities migrations.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Error.Preview: Run a migration preview before applying changes.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Migration.Error.BackupM: No migration backup was found to restore.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScSound.Hint: Play an audio file when this activity is used.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScSound.Fiel: Audio source played after the activity is used.
Choose whether the sound plays locally or is broadcast by a GM.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScSound.Warn: Choose an audio file before using this activity.
Only a GM can play this sound for everyone. Playing locally instead.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMacro.Hint: Run a selected world macro or inline code stored on this activity after it is used.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMacro.Fiel: Choose a world macro or inline code stored on this activity.
Macro executed with activity, item, actor, usage, dialog, message, results, speaker, and targets in scope.
Inline code is stored on this activity and runs only for GM users when the activity is used.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMacro.Warn: Choose a world macro before using this activity.
You do not have permission to execute the selected macro.
Only a GM can execute inline macro code.
Add inline macro code before using this activity.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScHook.Title: SC Hook
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScHook.Hint: Emit a configured hook or call a callback explicitly exposed by another module.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScHook.Field: Choose whether this activity emits a hook or calls a whitelisted module callback.
Hook emitted with activity, item, actor, usage, dialog, message, results, speaker, and targets in the payload.
Named callback in the target module whitelist.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScHook.Warni: Configure a valid hook name before using this activity.
Configure a module callback exposed through the SC - More Activities callback whitelist.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScChain.Hint: Execute selected activities on this item in order with loop protection.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScChain.Fiel: Activity ids on this item, one per line or separated by commas.
Maximum nested chain depth before execution is blocked.
Continue to the next target if a child activity is missing or throws an error.
Stop the chain when a child activity returns no result.
Do not post usage cards for chained child activities. Roll messages and the chain's own card are still posted.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScChain.Warn: Add at least one activity id before using this chain.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScConditiona: Route between this item's activities using conditions, rolls, and manual choices.
Fix these configuration issues before using this flow:
The step where the flow begins. Add a step below if this list is empty.
Maximum nested chain depth before execution is blocked. Shared with SC Chain.
Stop when a child activity is canceled
End the flow when a step's activity returns no result. Canceling a roll or a choice always ends the flow.
Keep routing when a step's activity is missing or throws an error.
Do not post usage cards for activities executed by flow steps. Roll messages and the conditional chain's own card are still posted.
No steps yet. Use the + button to add the first step.
The configured activity no longer exists on this item.
Actor data path, operator, and expected value or deterministic formula (e.g. @abilities.con.mod, @scLast.roll.sum).
Path on the most recent child activity result, plus an operator and expected value.
Type any path on the raw result object (e.g. roll.totals.1). The value field accepts numbers, true/false, text, or formulas — including @scLast.* references to the previous result.
Choose an activity above to narrow this list. Pick "Custom path…" to type any path.
Results shown for {activity} [{type}]. Pick "Custom path…" to type any path.
Number or deterministic formula (e.g. 8 + @prof, 10 + @scLast.roll.dice.count). The roll succeeds when the total meets it.
No value paths yet. Use the + button to add one.
Paths cannot overlap. Use a range such as 5..10 for an inclusive interval.
No options yet. Use the + button to add one.
The value returned by the last SC Macro activity.
The value returned by the last SC Macro activity.
true when the macro explicitly returned a value.
true when the last activity succeeded (attack hit, check passed, contest won).
true when the last activity failed (attack missed, check failed).
Total of the last activity's main roll (attack total, first damage part…).
true when the main roll was a critical.
true when the main roll was a fumble (natural 1).
The DC or armor class the main roll was compared against.
true when the last activity was canceled before finishing.
Internal id of the activity that produced this result.
Type of the activity that produced this result (attack, damage, save…).
Display name of the activity that produced this result.
Id of the item that owns the source activity.
UUID of the item that owns the source activity.
UUID of the actor that used the source activity.
Id of the chat message created by the last activity (empty when none).
How many document updates the last activity applied.
How many active effects the last activity created.
How many measured templates the last activity placed.
Total of the first roll, modifiers included. For damage, this is the first damage part.
Sum of every roll made by the last activity — e.g. the total damage across all damage parts.
Total of an individual roll. Use roll.totals.1, roll.totals.2… for the next rolls.
How many separate rolls the last activity made.
true when the roll met its target number.
true when the roll missed its target number.
true when the roll was a critical.
true when the roll was a fumble (natural 1).
The DC or armor class the roll was compared against.
The formula that was rolled (e.g. 1d20 + 5). Compare with = or includes.
Ability key used by the roll (str, dex, con…).
Skill key used by the roll (ath, ste…).
Tool key used by the roll.
Sum of the dice only, ignoring flat modifiers — the 2d6 part of 2d6 + 4.
How many dice were kept across all rolls.
Face rolled on the first kept die. Use roll.dice.values.1… for the next dice.
Highest face rolled among the kept dice.
Lowest face rolled among the kept dice.
true when the attack hit its target (or rolled a critical).
true when the attack missed its target.
Total of the attack roll, modifiers included.
true when the attack roll was a critical.
true when the attack roll was a fumble (natural 1).
Armor class the attack was compared against (single target only).
true when the grant's gating check succeeded.
How many documents (items, effects…) the grant created.
How many documents the grant updated.
UUID of the actor affected by the activity.
DC of the grant's gating check.
Total rolled on the grant's gating check.
true when the child activity was canceled before finishing.
Why the activity was canceled (e.g. child-canceled, roll-canceled).
Who won: "initiator", "defender", or empty on a tie.
true when the contest ended in a tie.
How many attempts were made (rerolls on ties).
Who won: "initiator", "defender", or empty on a tie.
true when the contest ended in a tie.
Total rolled by the contest initiator.
UUID of the actor that started the contest.
UUID of the token that started the contest.
Total rolled by the contest defender.
A step is missing its internal id. Remove and recreate it.
Choose the first step of the flow.
The first step ({ref}) no longer exists.
Step "{node}" points to a missing activity ({ref}).
Step "{node}" routes to a missing step ({ref}).
Step "{node}" needs a custom roll formula.

Step "{node}" needs at least one value path.
Step "{node}" has a value path without a key. Remove and recreate it.
Step "{node}" has duplicated value path keys ({ref}).
Step "{node}" has overlapping value paths: {ref}. Adjust their limits so one value cannot match both paths.
Step "{node}" has an invalid range ({ref}). Use two numbers separated by .., for example 5..10.

Could not open the choice dialog.

SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScContest.Hi: Resolve a contested roll between the activity user and a defender.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScContest.Fi: Choose whether the defender is the selected target or the same actor.
Choose how equal totals are resolved.
Choose the d20 test used by this side of the contest.
Ability used for this check or saving throw.
Skill used for this contested roll.
Formula evaluated with this participant actor roll data.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScContest.Wa: This contest needs an initiating actor.
Select exactly one target for this contest.
The selected target has no actor.
You do not have permission to roll for the defender.
Could not request the defender roll: {error}
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScContest.Er: Contest roll did not produce a valid total.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScGrant.Hint: Grant configured items to the activity actor or to the currently targeted token actor.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScGrant.Fiel: Grant items to the activity actor or to the currently targeted token actor.
Drop an Item or Roll Table, or paste a UUID
Drag & drop items or roll tables from the sidebar or a compendium. Roll table sources draw items from the table; the quantity is the number of draws.

The first field of each row is the quantity. It supports formulas with actor, item, and activity roll data.
Examples: 2, 1d4, @prof, @abilities.str.mod, @attributes.hp.max, @details.level.
Formulas are rolled when the activity is used and shown in the chat card.
For roll tables, the quantity is the number of draws.
No grant sources configured yet. Drop an item or roll table here, or use the + button.
If set, the recipient must pass this check to receive the items. Skill checks use the skill's default ability.
Pick "Fixed DC or formula" to type a value such as 15 (or a formula like 8 + @prof + @abilities.int.mod), or derive the DC from one of the activity actor's abilities.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScGrant.Warn: This grant activity needs an actor.
You do not have permission to grant items to this actor.
Add at least one granted item before using this activity.
This item is already in the granted items list.
Target exactly one token to receive granted items.
The selected target token does not have a valid actor.
No valid items could be resolved for this grant activity.
{count} result(s) from "{table}" did not resolve to items and were ignored.
Could not roll the configured grant check.
Invalid quantity formula "{formula}". Keeping the previous value.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScGrant.Info: {actor} failed the check (DC {dc}). No items were granted.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScAdvancemen: Trigger selected dnd5e advancements from a source item the actor already owns.
Paste or drop an item UUID
Use an actor item or compendium item that contains dnd5e advancements.

Select at least one advancement entry before using this activity.
The actor does not currently own the source item: {item}

SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.Canvas.Field: Choose which tokens this activity affects.
Maximum number of selected tokens affected by this activity.
Round the destination to the nearest grid position.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.Canvas.Warni: A GM must execute this canvas operation.
Open an active scene before using this activity.
Select or place the activity actor token on the scene first.
Select at least one target token.
An active GM is required for this canvas operation.
Could not request GM canvas operation: {error}
The canvas operation request is no longer valid.
You do not have permission to use this activity.
You do not have permission to move one or more selected tokens.
The requested canvas position is invalid.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScTeleport.H: Choose teleport targets and place their destination on the canvas.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScTeleport.F: Choose the token used as the teleport destination point.
X and Y offset from the destination in scene distance units.
Allow the activity actor token to be selected as a teleport target.
When maximum targets is 1, skip selection and teleport the activity actor.
Maximum distance from the activity actor for eligible teleport targets. Use 0 for no limit.
Maximum distance from the activity actor for the destination point. Use 0 for no limit.
Keep selected tokens in the same relative formation around the clicked destination.
Spacing radius used when not keeping the original arrangement.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScTeleport.W: Target one token to use as the teleport destination.
Destination is outside the teleport range.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScTeleport.A: Click the canvas destination for the selected tokens.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMovement.H: Push or pull selected tokens from the activity actor.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMovement.F: Push tokens away from the activity actor or pull them toward it.
Movement distance in scene distance units.
Maximum distance from the activity actor. Use 0 for no limit.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScMovement.W: No selected tokens are within this movement range.
Choose a movement direction for the self target.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.Title: SC Wall
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.Hint: Create walls by placing points on the canvas.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.Field: Wall endpoints are placed directly on the canvas.
Choose whether points create a continuous wall, a circle, or separated panels.
Choose whether one-way walls face both ways, toward the activity actor, away from it, or are chosen during placement.
Length of each panel in scene distance units. Formulas are supported.
Gap between panels in scene distance units. Formulas are supported.
Maximum panel count per wall. Leave empty for unlimited. Formulas are supported.
Maximum distance from the activity actor for wall points and generated segments. Use 0 for no limit. Formulas are supported.
Maximum wall length in scene distance units. Use 0 for no limit. Formulas are supported.
Maximum number of separate walls that can be placed. Formulas are supported.
Allow non-GM users who own the activity actor to request wall creation through an active GM.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.Warni: Only a GM can create walls with this activity.
The requested wall coordinates are invalid.
No selected tokens are within this wall range.
Need at least two points to create a wall.
No valid wall segments could be created.
Adding this point would exceed the maximum wall length of {length}.
You can place at most {count} wall(s).
Wall placement was cancelled because the active scene changed.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.App.I: Click the center point, then click or drag to the edge point.
Click points for the panel path. Panels use size {panelSize} and max {maxPanels}.
Click each point in order along the wall path.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.App.P: Click the canvas to place wall points. Right-click to stop placement.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Activities.ScWall.App.R: Click the canvas to place the reference point.
SCMOREACTIVITIES.Warning.UnsupportedSyst: SC - More Activities only supports the dnd5e system.
